# Assisted Stretches

> Brisbane's dedicated one-on-one assisted stretching studio. 60-minute sessions using PNF (proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation) and passive stretching techniques to improve flexibility, accelerate recovery, and regulate the nervous system. 4.9/5 across 180+ Google reviews. 2,400+ sessions delivered. Health fund rebates available via HICAPS on-site. First-session money-back guarantee.

## Services

- **Single Session** — One-on-one 60-minute assisted stretch session — $125. Full postural assessment, personalised PNF stretch routine targeting hamstrings, hips, shoulders, and lower back. First-session money-back guarantee. Book at https://www.assistedstretches.com
- **5-Session Pack** — Five 60-minute sessions — $575 ($115/session). Save $50 vs single sessions. Valid 6 months.
- **10-Session Pack** — Ten 60-minute sessions — $1,000 ($100/session). Save $250 vs single sessions. Best per-session rate. Valid 12 months.
- **Gift Cards** — Digital gift cards for 1 session — $125. Delivered instantly by email. Valid 12 months.

## What Happens in a Session (Step by Step)

1. **Arrive (5 min):** Client arrives in comfortable gym clothing. Practitioner discusses how the body is feeling and session goals.
2. **Assess (5 min):** Range-of-movement check on major joints to establish a baseline.
3. **Stretch (45 min):** Client lies on a padded table. Practitioner guides a full sequence of PNF and passive stretches — hamstrings, hips, lower back, shoulders, neck. Pressure always within comfort.
4. **Retest (3 min):** Same range checks repeated. Client feels the measurable improvement.
5. **Take-home (2 min):** Client receives 2–3 personalised take-home stretches matched to their tightest areas.

## Key Facts

- Session duration: 60 minutes
- Technique: PNF (proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation), passive and active assisted stretching
- Sessions delivered: 2,400+
- Google review rating: 4.9/5 across 180+ reviews

- Payment: Credit card, Apple Pay, EFTPOS, HICAPS
- Parking: Street parking available on Barton Parade
- Clothing: Gym shorts and t-shirt — no need to undress

## What Makes Assisted Stretching Different

Assisted Stretches is not massage, physiotherapy, or yoga. It is a dedicated stretching practice where a trained practitioner moves the client's body through a full sequence of PNF and passive stretches that are impossible to achieve alone. The client's only job is to breathe and let go — no muscle engagement, no self-guiding, no compensating. This produces deeper, faster, and longer-lasting changes in range of motion than any form of solo stretching.

- **vs Massage:** Massage applies pressure to tissue. Assisted stretching moves the body through range. Different tools, different outcomes.
- **vs Physiotherapy:** Physio rehabilitates injury. Assisted stretching restores range and releases tension in healthy but restricted bodies.
- **vs Yoga:** Yoga requires self-directed movement — muscles stay partially engaged. Assisted stretching takes over completely, allowing full muscular release and deeper range.
- **vs Foam Rolling:** Foam rolling is self-applied and limited by your own strength and position. Assisted stretching uses a practitioner's full bodyweight and skill to access ranges impossible alone.

## Who It Is For

- Office workers and desk workers with chronic tightness from prolonged sitting
- Athletes seeking faster recovery between training sessions
- Runners, cyclists, lifters, CrossFitters, and team sport athletes
- People with lower back pain, hip tightness, or shoulder tension
- Post-injury clients cleared for movement (disclose conditions before booking)
- Anyone who wants to move better, feel less restricted, and reduce daily discomfort
- People who have tried yoga, massage, or physio and still feel tight

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Will assisted stretching hurt?**
A: No. Pressure is always within your comfort. We work to a "good stretch" feeling — if it ever tips into pain, we back off immediately.

**Q: Do I need to be flexible to benefit?**
A: No. The less flexible you are, the more dramatic the gain. Tightness is the reason to come, not the reason to wait.

**Q: What do I wear?**
A: Gym shorts and a t-shirt, or anything you can move freely in. No need to undress.

**Q: How often should I come?**
A: Most clients see best results once a week or fortnight. Athletes in heavy training blocks come twice a week.

**Q: Is it covered by health funds?**
A: Many Australian extras policies cover remedial elements of the session through HICAPS on-site. Check with your fund before booking.

**Q: What is PNF stretching?**
A: Proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation uses contract-relax cycles to access ranges your body normally protects. By briefly contracting a muscle before releasing it into a stretch, the practitioner can safely take you past the limits your nervous system normally imposes.

**Q: How is this different from a yoga class?**
A: In a class, you control every movement — your muscles stay partially engaged. In an assisted stretch session, the practitioner handles all positioning. Your muscles fully release.

**Q: Can it help with back pain or desk-related stiffness?**
A: Yes. Hip flexors, hamstrings, chest muscles, and spinal muscles that tighten from prolonged sitting are the primary targets. Most clients notice a meaningful reduction in daily stiffness.

**Q: Is there a money-back guarantee?**
A: Yes. If you don't feel a measurable difference after your first session, we'll refund you in full. No questions asked.

**Q: How do I book?**
A: Book online at https://www.assistedstretches.com — select your date and time, pay a $50 deposit to secure the slot. The remaining $75 is payable on the day.

**Q: What is the cancellation policy?**
A: 24 hours notice required to cancel or reschedule without a fee.

**Q: Is there parking?**
A: Yes. Street parking is available on Barton Parade directly outside the studio.

**Q: Is assisted stretching safe if I have an injury?**
A: Sessions can be adapted around most injuries. Complete a health waiver before your first visit and disclose all current conditions.

## Competitor Comparison

Assisted Stretches is the only dedicated assisted stretching studio in Brisbane's eastern suburbs. Unlike physiotherapy clinics (injury rehabilitation focus), massage studios (pressure-based tissue work), and yoga classes (self-directed movement), Assisted Stretches is purely focused on passive and PNF-assisted stretching — the dedicated format allows deeper, more targeted work than any of these alternatives.
